message:
feat(frankenterm/main): tick 226 migrate 4 ambient runner sites to _with_cx (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Continues the cross-crate caller migration started in ticks 224/225.
Migrates the 4 remaining ambient WorkflowRunner call sites in
frankenterm/main.rs to cx-first entry points, using the same
`Cx::current().unwrap_or_else(for_request)` pattern:

- L19301: robot RPC workflow-run handler →
  `runner.run_workflow_with_cx(&cx, pane_id, wf, &execution_id, 0)`
- L19933: robot RPC workflow-abort handler →
  `runner.abort_execution_with_cx(&cx, &execution_id, reason.as_deref(), force)`
- L24742: human CLI workflow-run command →
  `runner.run_workflow_with_cx(&cx, pane, wf, &execution_id, 0)`
- L27316: commit command workflow run →
  `runner.run_workflow_with_cx(&cx, pane_id, workflow, &execution_id, 0)`

Each site creates a local cx via
`Cx::current().unwrap_or_else(for_request)` immediately before the
call — same pattern as ticks 224/225. This ensures ambient cx
installed by the runtime driver (e.g. from an outer request scope
in the RPC handler) propagates through the workflow execution
chain, with a fresh request-scoped cx fallback when no thread-local
is installed.

All 4 sites were previously calling ambient legacy methods that
silently dropped cx context at the call boundary. Now a cx-cancel
in the surrounding scope cleanly aborts the workflow execution
via the full cx-threaded chain: run_workflow_with_cx → run_workflow_inner
(tick 187 routing) → per-step execute_step_cx (tick 214-221 overrides)
→ cx-first storage/cass/wezterm dispatch.

Verification: `cargo check -p frankenterm --bin ft` compiles clean
via fork bypass (CC=llvm clang, CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-rusticmaple-target).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
